element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cub Simba's primary combat mechanic, low skill floor
Replenishable meter-based ability introduced alongside jumping
Resource discovery and management, basic pickups

element_introduction_order.element: Monkey puzzle complexity (expanded in Level 2: Can't Wait to be King)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Puzzle-platforming hybrid, difficulty spike to meet rental-game time requirements
Time-pressure mini-game with spider avoidance, rewards lives/continues
Reflex-based collection with failure state (bad bug), rewards lives/continues

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Unknown - mid to late levels likely
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Instant-death hazard requiring timing and evasion
Mechanic reset: replaces jump-on-enemies with scratching, mauling, throws
Expanded combat toolkit replacing cub's simpler mechanics
Permanent upgrade progression, health/roar meter expansion
Game-over safety net allowing mid-run recovery
Climactic narrative encounter, ultimate skill test

typical_relief_triggers: Bonus stage entry (reprieve from main platforming)
typical_tension_triggers: Hazard introduction (pits, water, lava, boulders, stalactites)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Front-loaded difficulty ramp in Level 2; alternating safe/hazard platforming sections; mid-game transformation creates tempo reset; bonus stage interruptions provide breath moments

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden bugs in specific level locations trigger bonus stages (Timon/Pumbaa); collection milestones (every 10 bugs for Timon) unlock progression rewards
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Rare meter-extension bugs hidden in specific levels for permanent stat upgrades; black spiders serve as negative-reward pickups

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic jump-on-enemies combat, low enemy density, roar ability as puzzle tool
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monkey puzzle spike in Level 2 expanded for rental-time padding; hazard introduction (pits, water, lava); bonus stages with time pressure; adult form transformation resets player skill temporarily
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced combat moves required; rolling boulders and falling stalactites; final Scar boss encounter
notable_spikes: Level 2 (Can't Wait to be King) - monkey puzzle expanded mid-development specifically to increase difficulty
Adult form transition - mechanic switch from jump-based to melee-based combat

notable_level_patterns.description: Hidden bugs in specific level locations, when collected, unlock Timon (time-limit bug hunting with spider avoidance) or Pumbaa (falling bug collection with bad-bug failure state) stages. Every 10 bugs eaten by Timon grants lives/continues/rare upgrades.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple levels (specific level locations not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Checkpoints distributed throughout levels allow respawn on life loss rather than level restart. Continues (Circle of Life icons) allow resumption from last failed stage rather than full restart.
Game splits into cub form (first half: jump-based enemy defeat, roar for puzzles) and adult form (second half: scratch/maul/throw combat). Corresponds to film narrative arc.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Between Level N and Level N+1 (exact point not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Monkey puzzle in Level 2 (Can't Wait to be King) was expanded late in development as difficulty padding to meet Disney's rental-game time requirements.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 2: Can't Wait to be King
